---
pagetitle: "Michael F. Joseph"
toc: false
---

::: {.site-hero}
[Michael F. Joseph]{.hero-name}

[International Relations · U.S. Foreign Policy · Applied Methodology]{.hero-fields}
:::

::: {.intro-grid}
![](images/profile/headshot.jpg){.headshot fig-alt="Michael F. Joseph"}

::: {.intro-copy}
**Assistant Professor of Political Science** (and Data Science, by courtesy), UC San Diego.

I study war, national security, secrecy, and modern technology. My research has
two strands. In the first, I integrate the historical and cultural determinants
of state motives into strategic models of great-power politics. In the second, I
explore how modern technology and secrecy create opportunities and challenges for
the intelligence community. This work has interested policymakers in Washington
for its insights on Sino-American relations and on the use of digital technology
in intelligence analysis.

Before my Ph.D., I worked as a foreign-policy consultant in Iraq, Australia,
Jordan, and the United States.

My book, *The Origins of Great Power Rivalries*, is forthcoming with Cambridge
University Press (2026). You can read my published work on the
[Research](research.qmd) page, and I summarize new articles in short videos on my
[YouTube channel](https://www.youtube.com/@Dr.MichaelJoseph).

::: {.connect}
[Email](mailto:mfjoseph@ucsd.edu) · [CV](files/cv/CV.pdf) · [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/@Dr.MichaelJoseph) · [Google Scholar](#) · [X](https://x.com/m0joseph) · [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/michael-joseph-82a5ba18/) · [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/michael.joseph.1048554) · [Bluesky](https://bsky.app/profile/profmickeyj.bsky.social)
:::
:::
:::
